J. COMPUTER APPLICATIONS IN ARCHAEOLOGY · 8(1) · 2025
Finding Hillforts at Scale
An end-to-end deep-learning pipeline that searches LiDAR across 180,000 km² of Europe
Landauer · Maddison · Fontana · Posluschny
England · Hesse · Molise

01 — WHY AUTOMATE
Too much LiDAR, too few hillforts
~1M
<0.05%
180,000 km²
image patches to scan in England alone (266 GB)
of land is hillfort — a needle in a haystack
searched by one automated workflow
The contribution isn't a new network — it's a working end-to-end pipeline that turns raw national LiDAR into a short list an archaeologist can actually check.

03 — PHASE A · BUILD THE TRAINING SET
From map points to labelled images
→
→

01
02
03

Atlas points
Cut vignettes
Prune bad sites
Labelled dataset
4,000+ hillfort centres, no outlines
768 × 768 m image per point
drop destroyed / built-over
579 forts + 20,000 negatives
Detection becomes ordinary image classification — and the elevation grid is fed in directly with min/max normalization, which trained better than rendered visualizations.
Raw DEM, no hillshade

505 forts · 15,156 neg.
+4%
accuracy from one self-cleaning pass — the model flags its own mislabelled training examples, no extra annotation needed.

06 — PHASE C · SCORE & VERIFY
The machine ranks; the human decides
AUTOMATED SCORING
HUMAN VERIFICATION
TP
Ranked candidates → open in GIS
candidate within 500 m of a known fort
↓

detection with no fort nearby
Cross-check LiDAR · maps · imagery

known fort the model missed
↓
Confirm or reject the site
Report F1 , not accuracy — the landscape is almost entirely negative.
~4 s
per site, first pass — 1,500 candidates triaged in 1 h 41 m

08 — WHAT THE PIPELINE FOUND
F1 ≈ 34–38% — but real discoveries
England
Hesse
Molise
F1 36%
F1 38%
F1 34%
1 new fort
12 + 4
15 / 100
previously unrecognized, confirmed in the Chilterns
forts recovered from outside the Atlas, plus 4 new candidates
of the top-ranked detections were genuine hillforts
Same false positives everywhere — hilltop villages, terraces, forest tracks all ring a summit like ramparts. That's why a human stays in the loop.

09 — TAKEAWAYS
A pipeline that scales, ports, and stays human-checked
WHY IT MATTERS
OPEN CHALLENGES
→
·
Detects complex, irregular structures — not just simple shapes
F1 of 34–38% leaves real headroom on precision
·
Fails where LiDAR fails — maquis and terraced hillsides
→
Runs at national scale — 180,000 km², the largest such search to date
·
Next: more regions, faster review, a shared benchmark
→
Ports to new countries with a light fine-tune, not a rebuild
